text.tspan: When I watched
that movie
I used to sit on the
edge of my seat and wait for the scene where he
breaches and the sensation always carried me
away. I did not understand that if I would only
find a way to encounter it in its world to make it
subside. That feeling also twisted and pulled
and nudged and scratched the minds of my
peers who also had dreams and drew pictures
that would not satisfy that hunger. To witness
in its full glory, the breaching whale had
nothing to do with wanting some freedom from
our own lives, but for the orca. But as a
foundational memory, the words to understand
it were limited, so in most the idea remained a
fire and burning desire to see a tormented and
beautiful creature yearning for freedom in
captivity until that glorious day when the orca
found the strength to jump over the jetty and
with its flight over the stones and the open sea
ahead and fluke arched, soaring while water
dripped off it and poured into my dreams.
